ldus.text:
ETHIOPIAN STATISTICAL SERVICE 
Statistical Bulletin 
 
 
 
YOUR RELIABLE DATA SOURCE 
 
www.ess.gov.et 
 
The Consumer Price Index (CPI), a key economic statistics, is surveyed, analyzed, and 
made available to data users on a monthly basis.   
The year-on-year, general inflation rate (annual percentage change) in the country stood at 
14.4 percent for the month of April EFY2017. A year earlier (in April EFY2016), the rate was 
23.3 percent. The month-on-month general inflation rate increased by 0.1 percent for the 
month of April-EFY2017. It was –0.7 percent in April EFY2016.  
General inflation encompasses of two main divisions and twenty-one groups of expendi-
tures. These divisions registered the following price changes during the month of April 
EFY2017 compared with April EFY2016. 
1. Summary  
Food & Non-alcoholic Beverages index 
The year-on-year inflation rate, also known as the annual inflation rate, measures the per-
centage change in overall price levels from one year to the same month in the previous year. 
Accordingly, the year-on-year inflation rate of the Food and Non-alcoholic component of the 
CPI, for the month of April 2017 EFY stood at 12.2%. This implies that the CPI of Food and 
Non-Alcoholic Beverages has, on average, increased by 12.2% compared to April EFY2016. 
The major contributing factors for the rise of food inflation for April EFY2017 was the ob-
served increase in the average prices of major food commodities such as bread and cereals 
(1.4%), vegetables (11.5%), meat (14.4%), Sugar, jam, honey & chocolate (19.0%),  Milk, 
Cheese and Eggs  (14.5%), Oils and Fats (25.6%), Fruit (14.9%),  and Food products n.e.c 
(20.1%). 
April EFY2017 
Issue No. 22/2017  
MONTHLY NEWS RELEASE 
 INFLATION REPORT ON APRIL, EFY 2017

2. Food & Non-alcoholic Beverages and Non-Food Inflation (Year-on-Year) 
The year-on-year inflation and the month-to-month inflation rates helps to gauge the cur-
rent inflation trajectory, as they capture short-term fluctuations in prices and provides an 
immediate indicator of inflationary pressures. Particularly, the month-to-month is also cru-
cial for monitoring changes in consumer prices and identifying any sudden spikes or de-
clines in inflation.

ETHIOPIAN STATISTICAL SERVICE 
Statistical Bulletin 
 
 
 
YOUR RELIABLE DATA SOURCE 
 
www.ess.gov.et 
 
 Beginning from April-EFY 2016 the magnitude of the inflation rate steadily declined overtime. In April 
EFY-2017 General inflation was 14.4% as compared to the one observed in April- EFY2016.

YOUR RELIABLE DATA SOURCE 
 
www.ess.gov.et 
 
As can be seen from the above figure, both the non-food year-on-year inflation rate and Food inflation 
rate increased in April-ETY2017  as compared to last month. The Non-Food inflation increased by 17.9 
percent during the same period. Particularly, Alcoholic Beverages and Tobacco increased by (16.5%); 
Housing, water, electricity & other fuel component (18.3%); Furnishings, household equipment & rou-
tine maintenance of the House (16.5%);   Health (10.5%); Transport (40.5%); Communication (7.4%); 
Education (23.2%);  Restaurants and hotels (18.3%) and Miscellaneous goods and services (18.0%) have 
contributed for the rise in non-food inflation rate in April EFY2017.  
 
Fig 1: Food and Non-Food Inflation (%, Year-on-Year), April EFY2016 - April EFY2017

Major sub divisions in the food and non-food commodities (year-on-Year). 
When we look at major division of food and non-food commodities, which holds large share in the 
commodity basket, the magnitude of inflation rate at April EFY2017 showed 12.2% as compared to 
the same month in the previous year.  From Food and Non-Alcoholic Beverage aspect, the highest 
weight is hold by Bread and Cereals, Vegetables, Other-Food products, Non-Alcoholic Beverage, 
and Meat; whose inflation at April EFY2017 is recorded  an increase for Bread & Cereals (1.4%); Veg-
etables (11.5%); Other-Food products (Food products not elsewhere classified) (20.1%); Meat (14.4%) 
and Non-Alcoholic Beverages & coffee (53.4%).  
Similarly, during April 2017 EFY the year-on-year inflation rate of the major Non- Food items of 
Housing, Water, Electricity, Gas, & Other Fuels (18.3%);  Restaurants and Hotels (18.3%); Alco-
holic Beverages & Tobacco (16.5%); Furnishings, Household Equipment & Routine Household 
Maintenance of the House (16.5%) and Clothing and Footwear (13.9%).

ETHIOPIAN STATISTICAL SERVICE 
Statistical Bulletin 
 
 
 
YOUR RELIABLE DATA SOURCE 
 
www.ess.gov.et 
 
Month-to-Month Inflation of Food and Non-Food categories: In April EFY2017, among the food components 
of the CPI, Meat increased by (0.4%), Fish and sea food decreased by (-2.4%), Milk, Cheese and Eggs increased by 
(0.4%), Oils and Fats decreased by (-1.0%),  Food products n.e.c decreased by (-0.5%),   Non-Alcoholic beverages 
and coffee increased by (1.4%) on the month to month basis. 
Table 3: General Inflation Rate (%, Month-to-Month), April EFY2016 - April EFY2017 
Page 5 
Issue No. 22/2017  
Month-to-Month Inflation Rate 
The month-to-month general inflation rate for April EFY2017 has increased by 0.1 percent compared to what was 
observed last month. During the same month, the food and non-alcoholic component of the inflation increased 
by 0.1 percent whereas the non-food component for its part increased by 0.2 percent.

Looking at non-food items, the month-to-month comparison was observed for Alcoholic beverages 
and tobacco decreased by (-0.8%), Clothing and Footwear (-0.2%), Furniture, Furnishing, household 
equipment and routine maintenance of the house  increased by (0.6%), Housing, water, Electricity, 
gas and other fuels increased by (-0.3%), Transport increased by (3.8%), Recreation and culture in-
creased by (1.8%), and Hotel and Restaurants (0.0%), Health increased by (0.5%), Communication 
increased by (0.3%), and Miscellaneous Goods and Services increased by (1.6%) showed an increase 
during the month.  
Fig 2: Food and Non-Food Inflation (%, Month-to-Month), April EFY2016 - April EFY2017

ETHIOPIAN STATISTICAL SERVICE 
Statistical Bulletin 
 
 
 
YOUR RELIABLE DATA SOURCE 
 
www.ess.gov.et 
 
Page 7 
The 12-month moving average figures provides a broader perspective on inflation trends over a longer period. 
As it is indicated in Table below the country level overall inflation rate (annual change based on 12 months 
Moving Average) was 17.1 percent in April EFY2017 as compared to the one observed in a similar period a year 
ago. Since EFY2016 inflation has been relatively eased in the country.  
Table 4. Overall CPI and 12-month moving average Overall Inflation rate April  EEFY2017

Dis-aggregated, 12-month moving average food inflation increased by 18.1 percent as compared to 
the one observed a year ago and non-food inflation rate increased by 15.7 percent in April EFY2017 
as compared to the one observed in April EFY2016.   
12-month moving average Food and Non-Food Inflation rate (April EFY2016 - April EFY2017)

ETHIOPIAN STATISTICAL SERVICE 
Statistical Bulletin 
 
 
 
YOUR RELIABLE DATA SOURCE 
 
www.ess.gov.et 
 
The average annual general inflation rate for the country stood at (26.6 %) in EFY2016 compared 
to (32.6 %) in EFY2015. The past five years are unfavorable for the households, due to high infla-
tion in the country. Specially, in the year EFY2014 and EFY2016, the inflation rate has been over 
30 percent for most months. Internal and external factors forced the inflation to be at higher lev-
el; COVID19, internal conflict, and Russia-Ukraine War were the major factors.   
Page 9 
Issue No. 22/2017  
Fig 3: Average Annual General inflation in Ethiopia since Ethiopian EFY2005 (Year-on-Year)  
4. Annual Inflation trend  
Relatively low and reduction in inflation rate was observed between 2005 and 2009. Since 
EFY2010 inflation rate stick to double digit.

ETHIOPIAN STATISTICAL SERVICE 
Statistical Bulletin 
 
 
 
YOUR RELIABLE DATA SOURCE 
 
www.ess.gov.et 
 
Page 10 
Issue No. 22/2017  
Fig 4: Average Annual Food Inflation in Ethiopia since EFY2005 (Year-on-Year)  
The average annual food inflation rate for the country stood at (28.1 %) in EFY2016 compared to 
(31.8 %) in EFY2015, about four percentage point reduction compared to last year figure. But 
since 2010 the inflation rate stacked at double digit.

ETHIOPIAN STATISTICAL SERVICE 
Statistical Bulletin 
 
 
 
YOUR RELIABLE DATA SOURCE 
 
www.ess.gov.et 
 
The average annual non-food inflation rate for the country stood at (24.3 %) in EFY2016 compared 
to (33.8 %) in EFY2015. Non-food inflation movement, the past ten year divided into two. Between 
2005 and 2009 non-food inflation showed reduction and since then the rate demonstrate increas-
ing trend. This is could be attached to Birr devaluation against USD, Russia-Ukraine War, and in-
crease of international price of gasoline, as most of non-food items are imported or utilize import-
ed raw materials. Relative easing of the non-food inflation for EFY2016 is observed as a result of 
tight monetary policy in place to reduce bank credit. 
Page 11 
Issue No. 22/2017  
Fig 5: Average Annual Non-Food Inflation in Ethiopia since EFY2005 (Year-on-Year)

Page 12 
Issue No. 22/2017  
• 
General Inflation: The highest level of aggregation of 
inflation which covers all the items within the scope 
of the inflation. 
• 
Food Inflation: inflation which covers all food items 
within the scope of the inflation. 
• 
Non-food Inflation: inflation which covers all non-
food items within the scope of the inflation. 
• 
Year-on-Year percentage change: The Yearly per-
centage change is the change in the index of the rele-
vant month of the current year compared with the 
index of the same month in the previous year ex-
pressed as a percentage. 
• 
Month-to-Month percentage change: The monthly 
percentage change is the change in the index of the 
relevant month compared with the index of the previ-
ous month expressed as a percentage.  
• 
Index reference period (December EFY2009=100): 
The period with which all other periods are compared 
(the period for which the value of the CPI is set at 
100).
